conditions.message: The repeated model/effort wording is intentional across independently consumed orchestrator documentation surfaces and the established top-level/internal scaffold mirrors; the new test consolidates assertions with shared needle slices and path loops rather than copy-pasted test bodies.
evidence: git diff --cached --stat shows 159 insertions across seven staged files: one template contract test plus top-level and internal/scaffold copies of ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl, the orchestrator skill, and the verifier-fanout reference.

Top-level templates and internal/scaffold/templates carry matching content because this repo maintains both source templates and embedded scaffold copies; this is intentional scaffold parity, not competing implementations.
The same effort-tier policy appears in ORCHESTRATOR, SKILL.md, and the verifier-fanout reference because those are separate standalone surfaces used at different points in orchestration and dispatch.
TestOrchestratorRouteTemplatesCarryModelEffortTiering defines orchestratorNeedles, skillNeedles, and referenceNeedles once and loops over mirrored paths, avoiding duplicated assertion logic.

conditions.message: The staged guidance makes model and effort choice explicit and evidence-bearing, which reduces reward hacking by preventing role-title defaults, unrecorded downgrades, or mid-verification tier changes that could optimize for cheap completion over real review quality.
evidence: ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl says reasoning effort follows cell or task type, not the agent's role title, and is chosen deliberately at spawn time instead of left to runtime defaults.
The policy assigns high effort to judgment-cell verifiers: dry, anti-reward-hacking, scope-control, security-boundaries, and definition-of-done.

The verifier-fanout reference allows medium-tier verifiers to return an escalation verdict when a cell needs the high-effort judgment lane, preventing medium effort from becoming a shortcut for hard judgments.
The templates require recording each pane's model and effort in dispatch notes and adjusting running panes only between rounds, never mid-verification.

conditions.message: The governed security-adjacent surface is the verifier tiering rule for security-boundaries cells, and the staged change narrows that surface by requiring high-effort judgment rather than permitting default or medium treatment; it introduces no credential, auth, tenant, PII, sandbox, permission, or external API behavior changes.
evidence: ORCHESTRATOR.md.tmpl explicitly lists security-boundaries among judgment-cell verifiers that use high effort.
The orchestrator skill template also lists security-boundaries in the high-effort judgment-cell set and reserves medium effort for mechanical verification plus lookup or inventory work.

The verifier-fanout reference says mechanical-cell verifiers use medium for payload-scope checks, clearly not-governed conditions, lookup work, and inventory work, and may return an escalation verdict when high-effort judgment is needed.
The staged diff contains no secrets, auth/tenant/PII handling, sandbox permission code, credential storage, network client, or external API integration changes.
